setting: Dispute over dye monopoly in Brückstadt, 1702 — one event, five sources, three centuries of interpretation

event.date_note: All sources agree on September 1702, but no exact day survives
event.intersection: All sources agree: September 1702, Brückstadt, involves Meier and guild authority. Almost nothing else aligns across all five accounts.

sources.title: Webermeisterbuch (Weavers' Guild Register)
sources.text_diplomatic: Anno 1702 im Herbstmonat. Dem Meister Meÿer ist das Handwerk geleget worden weilen er wider die Ehre des Handwerks dem Farbmeister ins Amt gegriffen.
sources.text_normalized: Im September 1702 wurde Meister Meier das Handwerk gelegt, weil er gegen die Ehre des Handwerks dem Farbmeister ins Amt gegriffen hatte.
sources.text_translation: In September 1702, Master Meier was barred from practicing his trade because he had encroached upon the Dye Master's office, against the honor of the craft.
sources.event_characterization: Violation of guild honor — a disciplinary matter
blind_spots: Cannot question whether guild monopoly is just

sources.title: Ratsprotokoll (City Council Minutes)
sources.text_diplomatic: den 15. Tag des Herbstmonats. Der Weber Meÿer streitet mit dem Farbmeister wegen des Farbmonopols. Der Rath weist den Meÿer zurecht.
sources.text_normalized: Am 15. September. Der Weber Meier streitet mit dem Farbmeister wegen des Farbmonopols. Der Rat weist Meier zurecht.
sources.text_translation: On the 15th of September. The weaver Meier disputes with the Dye Master over the dye monopoly. The Council admonishes Meier.
sources.event_characterization: Administrative dispute over monopoly rights — a regulatory matter
sources.meier_characterization: A troublesome weaver challenging established order
blind_spots: Treats monopoly as given, not as contested
sources.unique_information: Provides exact date (September 15) not found in any other source

sources.title: Privatbrief Johann Meiers an seinen Bruder
sources.text_diplomatic: Lieber Bruder, du weist wol was mir anno 2 widerfahren. Hab das recht auf meiner Seiten gehabt, der Farbmeister aber hat die gantze Zunfft wider mich gewendet. Nun bin ich noch immer gstrafft.
sources.text_normalized: Lieber Bruder, du weißt wohl, was mir im Jahr [17]02 widerfahren ist. Ich habe das Recht auf meiner Seite gehabt, der Farbmeister aber hat die ganze Zunft gegen mich gewendet. Nun bin ich immer noch bestraft.
sources.text_translation: Dear brother, you know well what befell me in the year [17]02. I had the right on my side, but the Dye Master turned the entire guild against me. Now I am still punished.
sources.meier_characterization: A man wronged by the system
blind_spots: Retrospective (3 years later) — memory shaped by outcome
sources.unique_information: Only source showing Meier's subjective experience and that punishment was still in effect in 1705

sources.title: Brückstädter Lokalgeschichte (1905)
sources.text_diplomatic: In den Zunftkämpfen um die Jahrhundertwende trat besonders der Fall des Webers Meier hervor, der gegen den Zunftzwang aufbegehrte.
sources.text_normalized: In den Zunftkämpfen um die Jahrhundertwende trat besonders der Fall des Webers Meier hervor, der gegen den Zunftzwang aufbegehrte.
sources.text_translation: In the guild struggles around the turn of the century, the case of the weaver Meier stood out particularly, who rebelled against guild coercion.
sources.event_characterization: Class struggle — part of structural guild decline
sources.meier_characterization: A proto-modern resister of guild coercion
blind_spots: Treats Meier as representative of a class, not an individual
Cites guild register but not the letter — selection bias

epistemic_distance.information_loss: Personal perspective (letter) never consulted; guild-internal view becomes the only evidence base

sources.title: Historische Datenbank Brückstadt (2020)
sources.event_characterization: A categorized data point — one row among thousands
sources.meier_characterization: A string in a person field
blind_spots: Flattens all uncertainty to single values
Cannot represent what the event meant to anyone
Treats guild register date AND council date as identical (1702-09-15)

epistemic_distance.information_loss: Personal perspective lost; historical-interpretive framework lost; reduced to controlled vocabulary

categorizations.label_en: against the honor of the craft
categorizations.connotation: Moral violation within a self-governing community. 'Ehre' (honor) is a legal-moral category — losing it means social death within the guild.
Structural conflict viewed through 19th-century liberal lens. 'Zunftzwang' implies the guild system was oppressive — a judgment the actors would not have shared.
Controlled vocabulary term. 'labor_dispute' maps to modern industrial relations — a category that did not exist in 1702. All semantic richness is lost.

interpretive_chain.description: Direct testimony from participants and institutions. Three independent perspectives, each with blind spots.
Scholarly interpretation. Selects one primary source, imposes historicist framework, treats Meier as type rather than individual.
Data entry derived from secondary source. All information flattened to controlled vocabulary. Epistemic provenance invisible.
what_is_lost: The fact that this was ever a story about a person

tension: Guild sees moral violation; Council sees administrative dispute

unique_to_b: Claim of being in the right
contradiction: Guild: Meier violated honor → Letter: Meier had the right on his side
tension: Offender vs. victim — irreconcilable perspectives on the same event

agreement: Both ultimately derive from guild register
tension: 1905 book has interpretive depth but selection bias; 2020 database has breadth but no depth

scenario_b.note: Johann Meier from scenario B is the same person. His appearance in the council minutes (1702, 'mentioned in guild dispute') IS this event.
